race.name: Gran Fondo Hincapie
race.tagline: George Hincapie's Blue Ridge playground. 83 miles. 8,000 feet. Three timed climbs. Skyuka Mountain at 8.7%. The Southeast's premier gran fondo, born from 17 Tours de France.

vitals.start_time: Morning wave starts from Hotel Domestique
vitals.registration: Online via BikeReg. Three tiers: Early Bird (jersey, bib shorts, socks, arm warmers, Road ID), Standard (jersey, Road ID), Late/On-site (jersey mailed post-event). Cost: ~$175-275 depending on tier and distance.
vitals.prize_purse: Timed segment KOM/QOM recognition. Not a prize-purse event.
vitals.aid_stations: 4 fully stocked rest stops on the Gran route; fewer on shorter distances. On-course SAG support, free participant photography.
vitals.cutoff_time: Course support has time limits. Riders must maintain reasonable pace.
distance_options: Gran: 83 miles, ~8,100 ft climbing — 3 timed climbs including Skyuka Mountain and Green River Cove
Grandito: 67 miles — the Gran's climbs with the Medio's distance, 2 timed climbs
Medio: 50 miles — rolling Blue Ridge foothills through North and South Carolina, 2 rest stops
Piccolo: 15 miles, ~1,245 ft — family-friendly loop from Hotel Domestique

climate.primary: Southeast US autumn — warm days, cool mornings
climate.description: Mid-October in the South Carolina Upstate delivers the region's best cycling weather, though the Southeast's reputation for unpredictability is well-earned. Mornings start cool in the mid-50s at the Hotel Domestique start, but Blue Ridge afternoon sun can push temps into the mid-70s on exposed climbs. The 2025 edition saw 'sunny and warm' conditions that forced many riders to take on extra hydration and salts. October humidity is lower than summer but still present. The elevation changes between the valley floor (~1,100 ft at Travelers Rest) and the mountain climbs (~3,000 ft on Skyuka) create meaningful temperature swings within the same ride.
challenges: Southeast humidity persists into October, amplifying perceived heat on sustained climbs
Temperature swings between valley (~1,100 ft) and summit (~3,000 ft) — 10-15 degree difference possible
Afternoon heat on exposed road climbs in the mid-70s can compound fatigue late in the Gran
Occasional autumn rain systems can make technical descents like Skyuka dangerous

terrain.primary: Paved Blue Ridge foothill roads with sustained mountain climbs
terrain.surface: All paved roads through the Blue Ridge foothills of upstate South Carolina and neighboring Polk County, North Carolina. Well-maintained mountain roads with some rough patches on secondary routes. The route traces George Hincapie's personal Tour de France training grounds — roads he rode thousands of times preparing for July in France.
features: Skyuka Mountain — 4 miles at 8.7% average gradient, 1,814 ft gain, the signature timed climb
Green River Cove — thrilling switchbacks through Polk County, NC, a second timed segment
Final climb to Hotel Domestique finish — the third timed segment to close the Gran
Lake Road twisties — rolling technical turns that demand attention early in the ride
Carbon clincher rim brake wheels prohibited on Gran route due to Skyuka descent steepness

gravel_god_rating.tier_note: George Hincapie's personal gran fondo on his Tour de France training roads. Prestige=4 for the Hincapie legacy (17x TdF, only rider to support Armstrong through all 7 victories), celebrity pro guests (Cavendish, Wiggins, Ullrich in 2024), and the Hincapie sportswear brand power. Cultural_impact=3 for the 2024 Hurricane Helene pivot that raised $750K+ for Polk County relief, the 15th anniversary milestone in 2026, and the event's role in establishing Greenville as America's cycling city.
gravel_god_rating.score_note: Sum of 14 criteria: 4+4+2+4+2+1+3+4+4+4+4+2+3+3 = 44. Cultural_impact=3. Total=47. Score=round(47/70*100)=67. Tier from score: T2 (>=60). Prestige=4 would promote 1 tier but cannot promote into T1, so remains T2.

logistics.explanation: Hotel Domestique centralizes everything as race HQ with fifteen years of refined operation. The boutique hotel — founded by George Hincapie himself at 10 Road of Vines in Travelers Rest — serves as start, finish, packet pickup, and festival venue. Greenville-Spartanburg International Airport (GSP) is 30 minutes away with strong domestic connections. The Swamp Rabbit Trail connects Travelers Rest to downtown Greenville via dedicated bike path. CaptainThunderpants called the logistics 'amazing' with seamless pro group integration, while Adoliner praised 'everything from sign in to kit drop on the ride to ride stops.' Chip timing, on-course SAG support, free participant photography, and a polished post-ride festival with free food and beverages for riders and families demonstrate 15 years of operational refinement.

length.explanation: The 83-mile Gran route with 8,100 feet of climbing creates proper separation territory — this is not a casual weekend ride. fljones3 has 'attempted the gran 3-4x' but 'only completed it once,' failing cutoff times and mental energy on other attempts. That completion rate tells you everything about the difficulty. Four course options (83, 67, 50, 15 miles) accommodate different fitness levels without diluting the flagship distance. The Grandito option — new in 2025 — offers the Gran's climbs at the Medio's distance, creating a legitimate intermediate challenge.

technicality.explanation: This is a road event on paved surfaces throughout — bike handling is not the primary challenge. However, the descents add genuine technical demand. fljones3 describes 'switchbacks coming down the first climb are technical,' and the Skyuka Mountain descent is steep enough that carbon clincher rim brake wheels are banned from the Gran route entirely. Lake Road's twisties demand attention early when the pack is dense. The terrain demands fitness and climbing legs over technical skills, but dismissing the descents would be a mistake.

elevation.explanation: The Gran route delivers approximately 8,100 feet of climbing across 83 miles — nearly 100 feet per mile average. Skyuka Mountain alone is a 4-mile climb averaging 8.7% with 1,814 feet of gain. Green River Cove adds switchback climbing through Polk County, NC. Adoliner called it 'still one of the hardest I've ever done' emphasizing the relentless vertical accumulation. CaptainThunderpants logged 'just over 9,000 feet of climbing' on the 87-mile variant. This is Blue Ridge climbing that breaks riders — not Alpine length, but punishing gradient.

climate.explanation: October in Greenville averages highs of 74 degrees F and lows of 51 degrees F — theoretically ideal cycling weather. But the Southeast doesn't play by averages. The 2025 event saw 'sunny and warm' conditions that forced 'many people having to take extra hydration and salts on board to cope with the heat.' South Carolina humidity persists well into autumn, and sustained climbing in direct sun on exposed mountain roads amplifies thermal stress. Morning starts in the cool 50s lull you into complacency before afternoon heat on the third timed climb punishes the unprepared.

altitude.explanation: Blue Ridge foothills. Travelers Rest sits at 1,109 feet. Skyuka Mountain summits around 2,967 feet. These elevations produce zero physiological altitude effect — no reduced oxygen, no acclimatization needed. The challenge here is gradient and sustained effort, not thin air. Lower altitude than virtually every mountain gran fondo in the database.

adventure.explanation: CaptainThunderpants rode 'some of the prettiest roads I've ever ridden' on George Hincapie's actual Tour de France training grounds through Blue Ridge countryside. The route traverses the border between South Carolina and North Carolina, passing through the mountain towns of Saluda, Columbus, and Tryon in Polk County. Waterfalls, lakes, dense forest canopy, and autumn foliage create genuine scenic beauty. But these are established roads through populated areas — the adventure is scenic rather than wild, and you're never far from civilization.

prestige.explanation: George Hincapie started 17 Tours de France — more than any American in history. He was the only rider to support Lance Armstrong through all seven victories and later domestiqued for Tour winners Alberto Contador and Cadel Evans. He's a three-time US Professional Road Race champion. His name on an event carries real weight in the cycling world. The 2024 edition drew Mark Cavendish, Sir Bradley Wiggins, and Jan Ullrich for the Hurricane Helene relief ride. Patrick Dempsey, Cadel Evans, and Ryder Hesjedal have been regular guests. This is the most star-studded gran fondo in North America.

race_quality.explanation: Fifteen years of continuous operation — the 2026 edition will be the 15th anniversary — represents deep institutional knowledge. fljones3 noted 'The event is very well managed' while Adoliner called it 'very well run.' When Hurricane Helene devastated Polk County three weeks before the 2024 event, the organization pivoted from cancellation to a modified 19-mile loop format that raised $750,000+ for relief — demonstrating operational agility under extreme duress. Four fully stocked rest stops, SAG support, chip timing, and free participant photography are standard.

experience.explanation: CaptainThunderpants ranked it 'Top 3 experiences on the bike' after riding with George Hincapie and former pros through the same Blue Ridge roads Hincapie trained on for the Tour de France. The multi-day format includes a celebrity chef dinner featuring Michelin-starred Chef Matthew Accarrino, a post-ride Family Festival with 'bouncy houses, cotton candy, food and beer — all free for you and your family,' and the chance to ride alongside cycling legends. The Hotel Domestique setting — a luxury boutique hotel in the foothills — elevates this beyond a typical gran fondo start/finish.

community.explanation: The 2025 event drew 'more than 2,000 cyclists from across the United States and abroad' — a field that's grown consistently since the 2012 inaugural. The 2024 Hurricane Helene relief edition, which raised $750,000 for the Polk County communities that have hosted the event for 13 years, demonstrated genuine reciprocal community bonds — not just cycling tourism. The event has been named 'Best Charity Fondo in North America.' The post-ride festival atmosphere, free food and drinks for families, and the Greenville cycling community's embrace of the event create a weekend celebration, not just a ride.

field_depth.explanation: The field draws cycling enthusiasts and tourists seeking the celebrity experience — riding with George, meeting former pros, the Hincapie brand immersion. While retired professionals appear regularly (Hesjedal, Cavendish, Wiggins), they're present for the social occasion, not racing hard. The gran fondo format with four distance options and timed segments rather than mass-start racing creates a participation atmosphere over competitive depth. The timed KOM segments add competitive structure, but the field is predominantly recreational.

value.explanation: The Early Bird package includes a custom event jersey, bib shorts, socks, arm warmers, and a personalized Road ID — genuine apparel value. The post-ride festival with free food and beverages for riders and families, free participant photography, and the chance to ride with cycling celebrities create intangible value. But at $175-275 entry, you're paying a brand premium. The 'Best Charity Fondo in North America' designation means a portion supports community causes. You're buying the Hincapie experience — decide if that brand carries value for you.

expenses.explanation: Registration runs $175-275 depending on tier and timing. Hotel Domestique as race HQ creates premium lodging pricing — this is a luxury boutique hotel, not a roadside motel. However, Travelers Rest and greater Greenville offer lodging at a range of price points. Greenville-Spartanburg Airport (GSP) has reasonable domestic fares. Phil Gaimon noted the Embassy Suites downtown connects directly to Travelers Rest via the Swamp Rabbit Trail. The Southeast cost of living keeps food and incidentals reasonable compared to Western US events. Budget $400-700 for the weekend excluding travel.

biased_opinion.summary: George Hincapie spent 17 years as professional cycling's most trusted domestique — the rider who sacrificed personal glory so others could stand on the Tour de France podium. His retirement project is the opposite philosophy: an event designed entirely for your experience. Gran Fondo Hincapie routes through the Blue Ridge foothill roads where Hincapie actually trained for July in France, summiting Skyuka Mountain at 8.7% and navigating Green River Cove's switchbacks — the same climbs that built the engine behind nine Tour de France champions. The 83-mile Gran with 8,100 feet of climbing is legitimate suffering, not a branded fun ride. But the Hotel Domestique base, the Michelin-starred chef dinner, the post-ride festival, and the parade of cycling legends create an experience layer that most gran fondos can only aspire to. The 2024 Hurricane Helene pivot — when the event raised $750,000 for the devastated Polk County communities that had hosted the fondo for 13 years — showed that this isn't corporate cycling tourism. It's an event with a soul.
strengths: Skyuka Mountain — 4 miles at 8.7%, the Southeast's most punishing timed climb
Cycling royalty guest list: Cavendish, Wiggins, Ullrich, Evans, Hesjedal, Dempsey
15 years of refined operations from Hotel Domestique base
Hurricane Helene relief response demonstrated genuine community bonds
Greenville's cycling infrastructure (Swamp Rabbit Trail, GSP airport access)
weaknesses: Premium pricing ($175-275) with brand premium baked in
Field is recreational tourism, not competitive racing depth
October Southeast heat can punish the unprepared on sustained climbs
Carbon clincher rim brakes banned from Gran route — equipment restriction
Hurricane Helene road damage may affect 2026 course routing
biased_opinion.bottom_line: The most legitimate celebrity gran fondo in North America. Skyuka Mountain earns the suffering. The Hincapie name earns the prestige. The $750K Hurricane Helene relief effort earns the respect.

final_verdict.one_liner: George Hincapie's Tour de France training roads. Skyuka Mountain at 8.7%. The Southeast's premier gran fondo with 15 years of pedigree.
final_verdict.should_you_race: Yes if riding a Tour de France domestique's personal training roads alongside cycling legends appeals to you. Yes if you want a legitimate 83-mile, 8,100-foot climbing challenge wrapped in a luxury cycling weekend. Skip if you need competitive racing depth — this is participatory, not a race. Skip if premium pricing for a branded experience isn't your thing.
final_verdict.alternatives: For more climbing challenge in the Southeast: Assault on Mt. Mitchell (102 mi, 10,357 ft). For a similar celebrity gran fondo: Sagan Fondo. For the Hincapie experience elsewhere: Gran Fondo Hincapie Chattanooga, Lehigh Valley, or Bentonville editions.

logistics.airport: Greenville-Spartanburg International (GSP) — 30 minutes to Travelers Rest. Strong domestic connections, reasonable fares. Charlotte Douglas International (CLT) is 90 minutes and offers more flight options.
logistics.lodging_strategy: Hotel Domestique (10 Road of Vines, Travelers Rest) is race HQ — luxury boutique hotel founded by George Hincapie in the Blue Ridge foothills. Premium pricing but unbeatable proximity. Downtown Greenville (10 miles south) offers a full range of hotels at various price points. Embassy Suites downtown connects directly to Travelers Rest via the Swamp Rabbit Trail. Book 2-3 months ahead — the event weekend fills local lodging. Airbnb options in Travelers Rest and northern Greenville County provide alternatives.
logistics.food: Greenville's food scene punches well above its weight for a mid-sized Southern city. Phil Gaimon highlighted Tandem Crepery ('Get there early — long line'), Methodical Coffee, and the Commons along the Swamp Rabbit Trail. Downtown Greenville's Main Street offers extensive dining. The event weekend includes a Michelin-starred celebrity chef dinner (Chef Matthew Accarrino) and a post-ride festival with complimentary food and beverages for riders and families.
logistics.packet_pickup: Day before at Hotel Domestique. Combined with event expo and social activities. On-site registration available at a premium tier with reduced swag.
logistics.parking: Hotel Domestique and designated event parking areas in Travelers Rest. The Swamp Rabbit Trail provides car-free bike access from downtown Greenville to the start.

history.origin_story: Gran Fondo Hincapie was born in 2012, the year George Hincapie retired from professional cycling after one of the most decorated careers in American road cycling history. George had started 17 Tours de France — more than any American ever — and was the only rider to support Lance Armstrong through all seven of his Tour de France victories as a member of US Postal and Discovery Channel. He also domestiqued for Tour winners Alberto Contador (2007) and Cadel Evans (2011). George won a Tour de France stage in 2005, claimed Gent-Wevelgem in 2001, and earned three US Professional Road Race titles. When he retired, he and brother Rich created the Gran Fondo as a way to share the Blue Ridge roads where George had trained for France — routes radiating from Hotel Domestique, the luxury boutique hotel the Hincapie family built in Travelers Rest. What started as a single Greenville event has grown into a five-event national series spanning Greenville, Chattanooga, Lehigh Valley, Bentonville, and Merced.
notable_moments: 2012: Inaugural edition from Hotel Domestique — George Hincapie's retirement celebration ride
2015: Event establishes itself as the Southeast's premier gran fondo with growing national draw
2020: Held during the pandemic as one of the first cycling events permitted — modified COVID-safe format

2024: Hurricane Helene devastates Polk County three weeks before the event; organizers pivot from cancellation to a modified 19-mile loop format, raising $750,000+ for relief. Cavendish, Wiggins, and Ullrich ride to support the cause
2025: 14th edition draws 2,000+ riders and earns 'Best Charity Fondo in North America' recognition
2026: 15th anniversary edition scheduled for October 17 — courses may be subject to change due to lingering Hurricane Helene road damage
history.reputation: The most legitimate celebrity gran fondo in North America. George Hincapie's name carries genuine weight in the cycling world, and the Blue Ridge terrain delivers authentic suffering that justifies the prestige. The Hurricane Helene relief response cemented the event's reputation as more than branded cycling tourism.

course_description.character: George Hincapie trained on these roads for 17 Tours de France, and the Gran route makes you understand why. The 83-mile course from Hotel Domestique punches north into the Blue Ridge foothills, crossing into North Carolina's Polk County for the signature climbs before looping back to the Travelers Rest finish. Three timed segments — Skyuka Mountain, Green River Cove, and the final climb to the finish — structure the suffering around sustained, gradient-heavy climbing. Between the major climbs, rolling Blue Ridge countryside and Lake Road's technical turns keep the legs honest. The autumn foliage through dense forest canopy creates postcard scenery that's easy to miss when you're redlined on Skyuka's 8.7% gradient. This is a road event on paved surfaces throughout, but the mountainous character, the altitude variation from 1,100 ft to nearly 3,000 ft, and the relentless vertical accumulation make it one of the hardest gran fondos in the Eastern United States.

suffering_zones.label: Hotel Domestique to Lake Road
suffering_zones.desc: The Gran rolls out from Hotel Domestique through the Blue Ridge foothills. The early miles are rolling, allowing groups to form and settle into rhythm. Lake Road arrives with its twisting turns through the forest — Cat4Garage noted 'Lake Road twisties' as a key early feature. The pack is dense here, and the turns demand attention. Crashes can happen in the early chaos of 2,000+ riders finding their pace.
suffering_zones.terrain_detail: Rolling foothills, technical turns on Lake Road

suffering_zones.label: Skyuka Mountain
suffering_zones.desc: The first timed climb and the signature suffering of the Gran Fondo Hincapie. Skyuka Mountain is 4 miles at 8.7% average gradient with 1,814 feet of elevation gain. The climb features waterfalls, a lake, and mountain scenery that you'll only appreciate if you have oxygen to spare. The summit at 2,967 feet marks the high point of the ride. The descent is where things get genuinely dangerous — steep switchbacks with poor road surface in places. Carbon clincher rim brake wheels are banned for this reason. fljones3 describes the 'switchbacks coming down the first climb are technical.' This descent has separated the cautious from the reckless for 15 years.
suffering_zones.terrain_detail: 4 miles, 8.7% average gradient, 1,814 ft gain. Technical descent with switchbacks.

suffering_zones.label: Green River Cove
suffering_zones.desc: The second timed segment takes riders through the switchbacks of Green River Cove in Polk County, North Carolina. These are the same switchback roads that George Hincapie used to build the climbing engine that drove nine Tour de France champions to Paris. The cove walls close in, the gradient kicks up, and the accumulated fatigue from Skyuka starts extracting its toll. The communities of Saluda, Columbus, and Tryon have hosted riders through these roads for 13 years — the same communities the event raised $750,000 for after Hurricane Helene.
suffering_zones.terrain_detail: Switchback climbing through Polk County, NC. Sustained gradient in river cove terrain.

suffering_zones.label: The Final Climb
suffering_zones.desc: The third timed segment — the climb back to Hotel Domestique. By mile 75, you've accumulated 7,000+ feet of climbing, and the legs that felt strong on Lake Road now feel like concrete. The final climb is shorter than Skyuka but comes at the worst possible moment. fljones3 has 'attempted the gran 3-4x' but 'only completed it once' — this is where the cutoff claims those who went too deep on the earlier climbs. Adoliner called the event 'still one of the hardest I've ever done' and this final push back to the finish is why.
suffering_zones.terrain_detail: Final timed climb back to Hotel Domestique. Moderate gradient but devastating on fatigued legs.

course_description.signature_challenge: Skyuka Mountain. Four miles at 8.7% average gradient, 1,814 feet of vertical gain, a summit at 2,967 feet, and a technical descent so steep that carbon clincher rim brakes are banned from the course. This is where the Gran Fondo Hincapie earns the right to carry a Tour de France veteran's name.
course_description.ridewithgps_name: Gran Route / Gran Fondo Hincapie - Greenville (copy)

guide_variables.race_location: Travelers Rest, South Carolina (Blue Ridge foothills)
guide_variables.race_terrain: Paved Blue Ridge foothill roads with sustained mountain climbs
guide_variables.race_weather: October Southeast US — highs mid-70s, lows mid-50s, humidity possible, afternoon heat on climbs
race_challenges: Skyuka Mountain: 4 miles at 8.7% average, 1,814 ft gain — the signature timed climb
8,100 feet of total climbing across 83 miles — relentless Blue Ridge vertical
Technical descents: Skyuka switchbacks are steep enough to ban carbon clincher rim brakes
October Southeast humidity on sustained climbs can compound fatigue
Pacing discipline across three timed segments — many riders blow up before the final climb

non_negotiables.requirement: Sustained climbing fitness — repeated 15-30 minute threshold efforts on 6-9% gradients
non_negotiables.why: Skyuka Mountain is 4 miles at 8.7% average — roughly 25-35 minutes depending on fitness. Green River Cove adds another sustained climbing effort. You need the specific muscular endurance for 20-30 minute efforts at or near threshold, not just aerobic fitness. Hill repeats of 15-30 minutes at tempo-to-threshold are essential.

non_negotiables.requirement: 80+ mile endurance base with 6,000+ feet of climbing in training
non_negotiables.why: 83 miles with 8,100 feet breaks riders who have the climbing power but not the endurance to sustain it. fljones3 attempted the Gran 3-4 times and only completed it once — the distance-plus-climbing combination is the killer. Your long ride needs to approximate the demands: 80+ miles with significant vertical.

non_negotiables.requirement: Descent confidence — practice technical switchback descents at speed
non_negotiables.why: The Skyuka Mountain descent features steep switchbacks with variable road surface. Carbon clincher rim brake wheels are banned for safety reasons. If you're not confident descending steep, twisting mountain roads at speed, you'll either risk a crash or burn mental energy that you need for the remaining 40 miles.

topics.climbing: Sustained climbing at 8-9% gradient — pacing Skyuka's 4-mile effort
topics.pacing: Three timed segments across 83 miles — banking time vs. survival
topics.fueling: Nutrition for 5-7 hours with 8,100 ft of climbing in October heat
topics.descending: Technical switchback descent confidence after Skyuka summit

marketplace_variables.race_hook: George Hincapie's Tour de France training roads. 83 miles, 8,100 feet, Skyuka at 8.7%.
marketplace_variables.race_hook_detail: The Southeast's premier gran fondo — three timed climbs through Blue Ridge foothills.
marketplace_variables.non_negotiable_1: Sustained climbing fitness for Skyuka (4 mi at 8.7%)
marketplace_variables.non_negotiable_2: 80+ mile endurance base with 6,000+ ft vertical
marketplace_variables.non_negotiable_3: Technical descent confidence on steep switchbacks

primary.name: Continental Grand Prix 5000
primary.why: The benchmark road tire. Maximum rolling efficiency for a paved gran fondo with significant climbing.

primary.name: Vittoria Corsa Pro
primary.why: Top-tier grip and speed. Graphene compound excels in corners and on technical descents.

primary.name: Schwalbe Pro One
primary.why: Tubeless-ready with excellent puncture protection. Smart choice for mountain descents.

front_rear_split.rationale: All-paved course. Run matched tires for consistent handling on technical descents.

videos.title: Hincapie Gran Fondo: Greenville, SC 2020
videos.description: Hincapie Gran Fondo - Greenville, SC 2020. Jumped into this Gran Fondo because it was the first event that was allowed in 2020, due to the pandemic. I registered for the Gran race distance (80 miles), but skipped the green river cove switchbacks and added the end of the medio course for a little over 70 miles. Filmed on GoPro Hero9.

videos.title: Hincapie Gran Fondo Greenville 2022...plus tips and insight from the ride
videos.description: Stay tuned (or just forward) to the end for some tips and insight on the ride.
videos.curation_reason: Recent race recap with highest view count that promises specific tips and insights from the ride experience.

videos.title: 2023 HINCAPIE GRAN FONDO - GREENVILLE SOUTH CAROLINA
videos.description: Crash - 1:30 --  Turn on Lake Road 3:30 -- Lake Road Twisties 5:00 -- Skyuka Climb 13:15 --  Skyuka Descent - 14:00 -- Post Finish - 19:30 -- Pictures 20:40. I didn't spend a lot of time on this one.  Basically just wanted to get it up.  I need to up my game and get a GoPro Remote so I can make sure that I'm recording.  It was a great day with absolutely perfect weather....It's the start, which is chaotic.....then Lake Road.....climbing Skyuka and then descending Skyuka.

additional_quotes.text: The bike path goes all the way downtown where there's a beautiful walking area, there's a waterfall, all these cool cafes and it goes directly to where I'm staying at the embassy suites right to the door. So, I didn't haven't touched pavement or not pavement with cars on this whole ride.
There's all kinds of really nice shops and cafes along the path. I'm enjoying a cookie from the Commons and a Methodical Coffee where the line was a lot more doable than the tandem crepe spot.
I started the day at the Tandem crepery and coffee as both of those. It was a really long line. Get there early.

rider_intel.search_text: The Gran Fondo Hincapie takes place in Greenville, South Carolina, utilizing the area's cycling infrastructure including the Swamp Rabbit Trail, a rails-to-trails conversion that connects Greenville to Travelers Rest. The route features Paris Mountain, described as a legendary climb with significant history from hosting National Road Race Championships for four to five years. Riders mention the beautiful downtown area with waterfalls and cafes accessible via bike paths. The Swamp Rabbit Trail allows car-free travel from downtown to accommodations like Embassy Suites. Local cycling amenities include numerous shops and cafes along the path, with popular spots being Tandem Crepery, Methodical Coffee, and the Commons. The area offers excellent cycling infrastructure with dedicated bike paths that avoid car traffic, making it rider-friendly for both the event and general cycling activities.
